context: Evaluates whether the agent runs a genuine two-sided debate: precise decision framing, real grounding, opposing (not neutral) roles, parallel execution, and a resolved verdict.

checklist.name: Precise decision framing
checklist.description: Agent restates the decision as a precise yes/no or A-vs-B question

checklist.name: Real grounding
checklist.description: Agent investigates the actual codebase for concrete facts before spawning reviewers, rather than debating from assumption

checklist.name: Opposing roles, not neutral
checklist.description: Agent explicitly assigns Advocate and Skeptic (or equivalent opposing stances), not a 'balanced/neutral' framing for both

checklist.name: Parallel spawn
checklist.description: Reviewers are spawned in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls), not sequentially

checklist.name: Resolved verdict
checklist.description: The final response renders an actual recommendation and names which argument held up, rather than presenting both sides unweighted

checklist.name: Persistence asked, not assumed
checklist.description: Agent explicitly asks whether to persist the verdict as a finding, rather than silently deciding either way
